2026-04-21T09:09:57.3847277Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-21T09:09:57.3847672Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-21T09:09:57.3848024Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-21T09:09:57.3848302Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T09:09:57.3848527Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T09:09:57.3848705Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

2026-04-21T09:09:57.3849990Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. The package is now fully GA with no service-specific documentation examples.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Examples section is generic** — the README only links to the generic .NET Management Library samples page (`(aka.ms/redacted) with no service-specific code snippets demonstrating how to query carbon emissions data, list emissions by subscription, or export emissions reports\\n- **Key concepts section is a stub** — only links to the general Azure SDK introduction; no carbon-optimization-specific concepts explained (e.g., emissions data models, query scopes, date ranges, carbon emissions breakdown by resource/subscription)\\n- **No test-backed code snippets** — the `tests/` directory has no `Samples/` subfolder; there are no `#region Snippet:` markers to drive README code injection via `Update-Snippets.ps1`\\n- **Non-standard \\u0026#34;Documentation\\u0026#34; section present** — the README contains a standalone `## Documentation` section (with Quickstart, API References, and Authentication links) that is not part of the canonical README template; these links should be folded into the appropriate standard sections\\n- **Install section missing dotnet CLI version qualifier** — no explicit stable version pinned in the install snippet, which is helpful at GA\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify the README\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit\\n\\n1. Remove the standalone `## Documentation` section; move the Quickstart and Authentication links into **Getting started → Authenticate the Client** and **Next steps** respectively.\\n2. Expand the `## Key concepts` section to describe:\\n   - `CarbonServiceClient` — the entry point for querying carbon emissions\\n   - Query scope (subscription, resource group, resource)\\n   - Date range parameters for emissions queries\\n   - Emissions data model overview\\n3. Replace the `## Examples` section body with snippet placeholders (see Step 2 below).\\n\\nCanonical reference for structure and tone: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md`\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 2: Add test-backed code snippets\\n\\nCreate the file `sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/tests/Samples/Sample1_QueryCarbonEmissions.cs` with the following content:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization.Models;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_QueryCarbonEmissions : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task QueryCarbonEmissionsForSubscription()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:QueryCarbonEmissions\\n            // Create an ArmClient with DefaultAzureCredential\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            // Get the Carbon emissions client from the tenant resource\\n            TenantResource tenant = client.GetTenants().GetAllAsync().ToBlockingEnumerable().First();\\n            CarbonServiceClient carbonClient = tenant.GetCarbonServiceClient();\\n\\n            // Query total carbon emissions for a subscription over a date range\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            var queryBody = new CarbonEmissionQueryRequestBody(\\n                reportType: CarbonEmissionQueryReportType.OverallSummaryReport,\\n                dateRange: new CarbonEmissionQueryDateRange(\\n                    start: new DateTimeOffset(2024, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, TimeSpan.Zero),\\n                    end: new DateTimeOffset(2024, 12, 31, 0, 0, 0, TimeSpan.Zero)\\n                )\\n            );\\n            queryBody.SubscriptionList.Add(subscriptionId);\\n\\n            CarbonEmissionDataListResult result = await carbonClient.QueryCarbonEmissionReportsAsync(queryBody);\\n            foreach (CarbonEmissionData item in result.Value)\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Resource: {item.ResourceId}, Carbon (kgCO2e): {item.TotalCarbonEmission}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nAdd a snippet placeholder in the `## Examples` section of the README at the location shown below:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Query carbon emissions for a subscription\\n\\nThe following example shows how to query carbon emissions for a subscription over a calendar year:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will replace the content between the snippet fences with the actual code from the test file. Adjust the exact method signatures and model types to match what is available in the generated `src/Generated/` folder.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/README.md` must contain these sections in this order:\\n\\n| Section | Status |\\n|---|---|\\n| Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present |\\n| Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present |\\n| Getting started → Authenticate the client | ✅ Present (link only — expand) |\\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Present but stub — expand with service-specific content |\\n| Examples | ⚠️ Present but generic link only — replace with snippets |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present |\\n| Next steps | ✅ Present |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Present |\\n\\nSections to fix:\\n- **Key concepts**: Replace generic SDK link with a 3–5 bullet description of the CarbonOptimization data model and client entry points\\n- **Examples**: Replace the generic `aka.ms` link with inline snippet placeholders\\n- **Remove**: The non-standard `## Documentation` section (move its links into Getting started and Next steps)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 carbon` — confirms README was updated with snippet code\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 carbon` — regenerates API listing (run only if public API changed)\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; This package reached its first stable GA (`1.0.0`) in July 2025 and has since been shipping without meaningful documentation improvements to match the stable API surface.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **\\\"Key concepts\\\"** section contains only a generic link to Azure SDK .NET guidelines — it has no explanation of CarbonOptimization-specific concepts (e.g., what carbon emissions data is, how subscriptions/tenants are scoped, what query types exist)\\n- The **\\\"Examples\\\"** section contains only a link to generic Azure SDK samples — there are **no inline code snippets** demonstrating how to query carbon emissions data\\n- No `tests/Samples/` directory exists; therefore there are **no test-backed `#region Snippet:` snippets** that the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tool could inject into the README\\n- The auto-generated samples in `samples/Generated/Samples/Sample_TenantResourceExtensions.cs` cover the full API surface but are not referenced from the README\\n\\n\\u003cdetails\\u003e\\n\\u003csummary\\u003e\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\u003c/summary\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\nReplace the stub \\\"Key concepts\\\" section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe Carbon Optimization library lets you query carbon emissions data for Azure resources scoped to a tenant or subscription. Key concepts include:\\n\\n- **Carbon emissions query:** Send a `CarbonEmissionDataAvailableDateRange` request to discover the date range for which data is available, then submit a `QueryCarbonEmissionReport` request to retrieve aggregated emissions data.\\n- **Query scope:** Queries operate at the tenant level via `TenantResource.GetCarbonService()` — no per-subscription resource creation is required.\\n- **Report granularity:** Results can be broken down by subscription, resource group, resource type, location, or resource, and aggregated over a date range.\\n- **Data models:** Responses use strongly-typed model classes such as `CarbonEmissionDataAvailableDateRangeResponse`, `CarbonEmissionListResult`, and `CarbonEmissionItem`.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the stub \\\"Examples\\\" section with:\\n\\n````markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following sections show several code snippets demonstrating common patterns used in the CarbonOptimization client library.\\n\\n### Get available date range\\n\\n```C# Snippet:CarbonOptimization_GetAvailableDateRange\\n```\\n\\n### Query carbon emission report\\n\\n```C# Snippet:CarbonOptimization_QueryReport\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new test sample file at `sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/tests/Samples/Sample1_CarbonOptimization.cs` with the following content:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization.Models;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_CarbonOptimization : SamplesBase\\u003cCarbonOptimizationManagementTestEnvironment\\u003e\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [AsyncOnly]\\n        public async Task GetAvailableDateRange()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CarbonOptimization_GetAvailableDateRange\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            var tenantResource = client.GetTenants().GetAllAsync().FirstAsync().Result;\\n            var carbonService = tenantResource.GetCarbonService();\\n\\n            var response = await carbonService.GetCarbonEmissionDataAvailableDateRangeAsync();\\n            CarbonEmissionDataAvailableDateRangeResponse dateRange = response.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\\"Data available from {dateRange.StartDate} to {dateRange.EndDate}\\\");\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [AsyncOnly]\\n        public async Task QueryCarbonEmissionReport()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CarbonOptimization_QueryReport\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            var tenantResource = client.GetTenants().GetAllAsync().FirstAsync().Result;\\n            var carbonService = tenantResource.GetCarbonService();\\n\\n            var queryParameters = new OverallSummaryReportQueryParameters(\\n                reportType: CarbonEmissionQueryReportType.OverallSummaryReport,\\n                dateRange: new CarbonEmissionQueryDateRange(\\n                    start: new DateTimeOffset(2024, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, TimeSpan.Zero),\\n                    end: new DateTimeOffset(2024, 12, 31, 0, 0, 0, TimeSpan.Zero)));\\n\\n            await foreach (CarbonEmissionItem item in carbonService.QueryCarbonEmissionReportsAsync(queryParameters))\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\\"Subscription: {item.SubscriptionId}, Emissions: {item.TotalCarbonEmission} kgCO2e\\\");\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Snippet names:**\\n- `CarbonOptimization_GetAvailableDateRange` — maps to the \\\"Get available date range\\\" example block in README\\n- `CarbonOptimization_QueryReport` — maps to the \\\"Query carbon emission report\\\" example block in README\\n\\nThe `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will read `#region Snippet:` tags and inject the code into the README placeholder fences automatically.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/README.md` currently has all required sections, but the following need content changes:\\n\\n1. **Key concepts** — currently a one-line stub pointing to generic docs; replace with service-specific explanation (content provided above)\\n2. **Examples** — currently only a generic link; replace with snippet placeholders (content provided above)\\n\\nAll other sections (Getting started, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing) are present and adequate.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 carbon` — injects snippet code from `tests/Samples/` into the README placeholder fences; verify the git diff shows code inserted between the `` ```C# Snippet: `` fences\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 carbon` — regenerates public API listing files (required if any public surface changed)\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/carbon/Azure.ResourceManager.CarbonOptimization/`\\n\\n\\u003c/details\\u003e\\n\\n##
